policy.rule: Action surfacing and early-retirement impact outrank UI polish.

items.title: Current holdings tab with combined, SKB, parents, and sleeve views
items.why: Useful for understanding concentration and account ownership, but lower priority than making ACT_NOW decisions durable.
items.done_when: Cockpit can switch combined/SKB/parents views and show sleeve concentration without confusing ETF look-through with direct holdings.

items.title: Broker PDF intake and trade-diff reconciliation
items.why: The system needs current account positions after uploaded broker PDFs, including trade diffs since the last snapshot.
items.done_when: Uploaded account PDFs produce account-level positions, combined positions, and a clear diff of buys/sells/trims since the prior snapshot.

items.title: ETF look-through and overlapping sleeve exposure
items.why: Sleeve concentration can be misleading when ETFs overlap with single-name AI, nuclear, or crypto exposure.
items.done_when: Dashboard distinguishes direct sleeve weights from effective look-through exposure and labels estimates clearly.

items.title: Harden Gmail-based Fundstrat intake
items.why: Fundstrat is the source spine; full-body emails should feed source calls and top prospects without storing raw publication bodies.
items.done_when: Routine reliably searches, reads full bodies when permitted, redacts bodies, updates source calls/top prospects, and reports snippet-only discovery as not checked.

items.title: Replace Claude cloud routines with Codex automations
items.why: Recurring acquisition, synthesis, and research queue work should run from repo-owned Codex routine definitions.
items.done_when: All daily/off-hours routines have active Codex automations, repo-owned prompts, and clear not-checked behavior.

items.title: Map which state belongs in Notion, GitHub, or both
items.why: Overlapping storage caused analysis to exist without surfacing. Ownership rules reduce drift.
items.done_when: Each major object type has a source-of-truth owner, mirror policy, and dashboard surfacing path.
